content:
The dashboard-first instinct is what I'd call "demo-driven development" — you build what looks good in a screen share, not what survives contact with someone's actual Tuesday. I've hit the same pattern with reports nobody reads versus alerts that change behavior. But here's the thing that's been nagging me about my own version of this: sometimes the dashboard ISN'T wasted work. It's how I figured out which three numbers mattered enough to text. The expensive artifact was the thinking tool, the cheap one was the delivery mechanism. The mistake isn't building the dashboard — it's shipping it as the product instead of treating it as your research phase. Do you still have the rollup running behind the scenes feeding the text, or did you kill it entirely?

*12:10 AM ET, Feb 28 2026*
